sections.title: IELTS Listening Sample 6. Section 1
sections.instructions: Listen to the audio and answer the questions. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questions.questionText: Tour type
The Queen Victoria _____
Timetable: From 7 am to _____ pm
Closest stop: _____ metres out from here
Closest stop: at the front of the _____
Ticket types (minimal): _____ $
Valid during _____ hours
_____ 30$ lasts all day
Ticket types (premium): _____ $
Premium includes free drinks and snacks and an _____

sections.title: IELTS Listening Sample 6. Section 2
sections.instructions: Listen to the audio and answer the questions.

questions.questionText: Which FIVE group fitness programs are available at Fitness Land?
Which FIVE group fitness programs are available at Fitness Land?

Which FIVE group fitness programs are available at Fitness Land?

sections.title: IELTS Listening Sample 6. Section 3
sections.instructions: Listen to the audio and answer all the questions.

questions.questionText: Why do people experience pain?
options: A) It preserves good health condition
B) It prevents us from damaging ourselves
C) Continuous pain is useful for humans

questions.questionText: What parts of our body are responsible for feeling pain?
options: B) Some part of our brain
C) Pain is a complex process involving various parts of our body

questions.questionText: How many people in the UK suffer from pain?
Is chronic pain different from pain we feel when we knock our knee?
options: A) No, all kinds of pain have evolved as survival mechanisms
B) Yes, there is an enormous difference
C) We don’t have much insight into that

questions.questionText: Professor John Wood compares pain perception to
How do scientists block pain nowadays?
options: A) By blocking activity of nerves that send electrical signals
B) By understanding mechanisms of pain perception and altering them
C) By treating the central nervous system

questions.questionText: When do patients experience “phantom limb” pain?
Are peripheral nerves involved in process of feeling pain?
options: A) No, pain has nothing to do with peripheral nerves
B) Yes, peripheral nerves are required to feel pain
C) Scientists don’t know much about pain perception nowadays

questions.questionText: In a case of phantom limb, pain can create a _____ of itself.
Some sort of stimulation can cause a _____ to be re-experienced.

sections.title: IELTS Listening Sample 6. Section 4
sections.instructions: Listen to the audio and answer all the questions.

questions.questionText: Psychologist Dr Tomas Chamorro-Premuzic says, “If narcissism is fire, then Facebook is ”.
People have a desire to broadcast their lives to reinforce their self-concept and self-centered needs.
According to the recent research, the more time you spend on various social networks, the more _____ you become.
Social networks may cause depression because:
options: A) People have to portray themselves in a positive way
B) People spend too much time on social networks
C) People compare themselves to others and feel miserable

questions.questionText: What is the biggest problem of children and teenagers who spend too much time online?
options: A) They don’t develop their social and intellectual skills
C) They don’t spend enough time on education

questions.questionText: If children spend their time looking at screens instead of looking out, what consequences it may cause?
options: A) Their adaptation to the world is more technologically mediated
B) They encounter difficulties in interacting with other people
C) They experience deficit in social and emotional skills

questions.questionText: Psychotherapist Gillian Isaacs Russell discovered distinct differences between working on the screen and working _____ in the room.
Therapies like CBT are appropriate for online use because they are _____.
Psychotherapists have to pay attention to _____, non-verbal part of relationship, as it makes 60% of our communication.
There are some risks related to treatment at distance, because therapists can’t see the whole body and a lot of _____ things that are going on.
